pattern: Each lowercase word is reduced to a 26-bit set of its letters, so two words share no letter exactly when their bitmasks have an empty intersection, turning the pairwise compatibility test into a single bitwise AND.
hints: Whether two words share a letter depends only on which letters each contains, not how many times.
Represent each word's letter set as a 26-bit number, one bit per letter present.
Two words are compatible when the bitwise AND of their masks is zero; over all such pairs keep the largest product of their lengths, defaulting to zero.

edge_inputs.description: every word shares a letter so answer is zero
two words overlap in one letter only
full-alphabet word blocks all pairs with it

stress.description: random lowercase words over a small alphabet to force overlaps
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(2, min(size, 120))
    import string
    words = []
    for _ in range(n):
        length = rng.randrange(1, 12)
        words.append(''.join(rng.choice(string.ascii_lowercase[:8]) for _ in range(length)))
    return [words]
stress.note: narrow alphabet makes disjoint pairs rare so the zero case and real maxima both appear
